_meta.note: 25th major-arc enrichment dip; PASS 2 of the multi-pass A2.5 mini-track (the LAST A-roundup unit). PASS-2 cut = THE WESTERLANDS RAID: after being crowned + relieving Riverrun (PASS 1), Robb carries the war west past the Golden Tooth into the Lannister heartland -- Oxcross (the night victory over Stafford's green host) -> the raid (Ashemark / the gold mines) -> the storming of the Crag (Gawen Westerling's seat) -> (EXISTING) the Jeyne-Westerling marriage blunder -> (EXISTING) the RW-upstream spine. NO NEW NODES: all cluster nodes exist; this is a WIRE + ENRICH dip (like PASS 1's Whispering Wood). The genuine GAPS: (1) the causal raid spine -- battle-of-oxcross + taking-of-ashemark were CAUSALLY ISLANDED (0 in/out causal edges) and storming-of-the-crag had its ENABLES-out to the marriage but no causal IN; the 3 new ENABLES wire king-in-the-north -> oxcross -> ashemark -> crag into the already-built marriage spine (storming-of-the-crag ENABLES robb-weds-jeyne-westerling EXISTS -- the seam closes). (2) the roster-EMPTY storming-of-the-crag node (0 participants, no LOCATED_AT) got its BOOK-cited roster from asos-catelyn-02:143 -- robb COMMANDS_IN + AGENT_IN (broke the gate with a ram), the Smalljon (jon-umber-son-of-jon) + Black Walder (walder-frey-son-of-ryman) AGENT_IN scaling parties, grey-wind FIGHTS_IN (killed a man at the Crag), rolph-spicer COMMANDS_IN (the DEFENDING castellan who yielded), LOCATED_AT crag. (3) jeyne-westerling HEALS robb-stark (she nursed his festering arrow wound -- asos-catelyn-02:143). (4) Oxcross gaps: lymond-vikary VICTIM_IN (named dead, acok-sansa-03:153), grey-wind FIGHTS_IN taking-of-ashemark (lit the islanded Ashemark hub's first role), and rickard-karstark KILLS stafford-lannister (the marquee Oxcross kill -- a true gap, no KILLS/KILLED_BY for Stafford existed; distinct from the existing rickard AGENT_IN role). WHODUNIT HONEST: 0 new SUSPECTED_OF (Lens B). The Spicer-marriage-trap is ALREADY WIRED (grey-wind OPPOSES rolph-spicer + sybell-spicer CONSPIRES_WITH tywin-lannister both EXIST) and its full AFFC reveal (Tywin's pardon / Rolph -> Lord of Castamere / Jeyne made barren) is the RW-upstream BETRAYAL MECHANISM = PASS 3 (logged a clean 7-edge PASS-3 thread list). NO TWOW. THEORY-GATED clean. NODE-DUP flagged (small-fix, NOT this dip): black-walder-frey vs walder-frey-son-of-ryman twins. The 3 ENABLES (E1-E3) carry verify:true (cross-segment / inferential preconditions) for fresh-verify; the rest are Tier-1 verbatim. PASS 3 = the unravelling (the Fords / Duskendale -> RW-upstream); A2.8 Davos/Sam residual closes the A-roundup.

edges.quote: Our forces still hold the stronghold at the Golden Tooth, and they swear he did not pass
edges.note: THE RAID SPINE (1/3). Once crowned King in the North and with Riverrun relieved (PASS 1), Robb became an offensive sovereign: he carried the war west past the Golden Tooth into the Lannister heartland and fell on Stafford's host at Oxcross. ENABLES not CAUSES -- the crowning + the freed army were the precondition; Robb FREELY chose the westward offensive (Tyrion is baffled it even reached Stafford: the Golden Tooth garrison 'swear he did not pass'). Antecedent = robb-proclaimed-king-in-the-north (the status-change that licensed the offensive war), keeping a clean linear spine camps->king->oxcross (battle-of-the-camps ENABLES robb-proclaimed-king-in-the-north already exists; chaining from the crowning avoids a double-hop fork). Tier-2: the sequence is established inferentially (Tyrion's surprise at the offensive reach), not a single explicit cause-sentence.

edges.quote: Knights were trampled to death in their pavilions, and the rabble woke in terror and fled
edges.note: THE RAID SPINE (2/3) -- lights the causally-islanded taking-of-ashemark hub. Stafford's raw green host ('apprentice boys, miners, fieldhands') was the only organized Lannister force between Robb and the Westerlands interior; its destruction at Oxcross laid the heartland open, and Robb's host swept on to take Ashemark and the gold mines (the Crag node's own wiki prose: 'After the Battle of Oxcross ... takes castles ... including Ashemark'). ENABLES not CAUSES -- the host's destruction was the military precondition; Robb's host freely chose to raid deeper. Tier-2 (the Oxcross->Ashemark sequence is canon but not a single cause-sentence).

edges.quote: At last word he was marching toward the Crag, the seat of House Westerling
edges.note: THE RAID SPINE (3/3) -- lights the causally-islanded taking-of-ashemark hub's OUT and gives storming-of-the-crag its first causal IN. Maester Vyman's report places the Crag as the next objective after Ashemark (the raven-to-Ashemark relay 'so they could send a rider after him' confirms Ashemark is the waypoint en route to the Crag). The raid rolled through Ashemark and the gold mines on to the Crag. ENABLES not CAUSES -- taking Ashemark positioned the host to reach the Crag; the storming was a distinct assault Robb chose to execute. The seam closes here: storming-of-the-crag ENABLES robb-weds-jeyne-westerling already exists. Tier-2.

edges.quote: we took it by storm one night
edges.note: Robb commanded the night assault on the Crag (the storming-of-the-crag node had ZERO participant roles before this dip). He led the storm and Ser Rolph yielded the castle to him. Dual role with AGENT_IN (E5): he both commanded and personally broke the gate.

edges.quote: I broke the main gate with a ram
edges.note: Robb personally broke the Crag's main gate with a ram -- a direct combat act, distinct from his COMMANDS_IN command role (E4).

edges.quote: Black Walder and the Smalljon led scaling parties over the walls
edges.note: The Smalljon (= jon-umber-son-of-jon, the Greatjon's son -- NOT jon-umber) led one of the two scaling parties over the Crag's walls. Direct participant role on a previously role-less hub.

edges.quote: Black Walder and the Smalljon led scaling parties over the walls
edges.note: Black Walder (= walder-frey-son-of-ryman, the alias-bearing wiki-canonical node -- NOT the black-walder-frey twin; node-dup flagged for small-fixes) led the other scaling party over the Crag's walls.

edges.quote: Grey Wind killed a man at the Crag
edges.note: Grey Wind killed a man during the storming of the Crag (Robb's own account; the same kill-tally line covers Ashemark E13 and the already-existing Oxcross AGENT_IN role).

edges.quote: I took an arrow in the arm just before Ser Rolph yielded us the castle
edges.note: Ser Rolph Spicer was the DEFENDING castellan of the weakly-garrisoned Crag who yielded the castle to Robb -- COMMANDS_IN on the defender's side (note the side: this is the losing/defending command, the complement to Robb's attacking command E4).

edges.quote: The Crag was weakly garrisoned, so we took it by storm one night
edges.note: The storming occurred at the Crag (Gawen Westerling's seat). The node had NO LOCATED_AT edge at all (baseline gap).

edges.quote: she nursed me until the fever passed
edges.note: Robb took an arrow wound in the arm storming the Crag; it festered, and Jeyne Westerling took him to her own bed and nursed him through the fever -- the nursing that led directly to the marriage (and, via the Frey slight, to the Red Wedding). Clean gap; HEALS (healer -> healed).

Ser Lymond Vikary is named among the Lannister dead at Oxcross in Tyrion's account (the node existed with no incoming edges). DEDUP: stafford/stevron/martyn VICTIM_IN + grey-wind/rickard AGENT_IN + robb COMMANDS_IN + LOCATED_AT already exist; this is the one missing live-node casualty (Rubert Brax / Crakehall / Jast have no clean single-character nodes).
Grey Wind killed a man at Ashemark (the same Robb kill-tally line). Gives the previously-islanded, role-less taking-of-ashemark hub its first participant role.

edges.quote: Lord Rickard Karstark drove a lance through his chest
edges.note: The marquee kill of Oxcross: Rickard Karstark drove a lance through Ser Stafford Lannister's chest. A genuine gap -- no KILLS/KILLED_BY/EXECUTES edge for Stafford existed; this dyad is distinct from the existing rickard-karstark AGENT_IN battle-of-oxcross role (same pattern as PASS 1's Jaime KILLS dyads coexisting with VICTIM_IN roles). stafford VICTIM_IN battle-of-oxcross + DIED_AT oxcross already exist.
